mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command adds the Cluster API chart repository.

steps.title: Install Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs from the specified release.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm, which are necessary for managing clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.5

```

> This command installs the Cluster API components in the kube-system namespace.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.5

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to define a new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your specific infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> This command lists all pods in the kube-system namespace to verify that Cluster API components are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may result in data loss for any clusters managed by Cluster API.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command uninstalls the Cluster API components.
> This command deletes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster resources. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o json > backup-nodes.json

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.5

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup current node configurations.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that the upgraded components are running.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.5

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not available in the specified version.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Cluster API CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the API server is not reachable or the Cluster API components are not running.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Cluster API pods with: kubectl get pods -n kube-system

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and referenced in your Cluster custom resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the kubeconfig or the API server's certificate.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure that the API server's certificate is valid.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the handling of cluster upgrades.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1alpha3' for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12.0 or earlier, ensure to update your Cluster custom resources to use the new API version 'v1beta1'.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster with a status of 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.5

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
